pages.raw_text:
"The Whirl o. tn Town." pre-
sented by local talent under the
auspices of the Woman's club last
evening scored big success. The
house was crowded. There is no
particular plot, but plenty of clean
comedy. The principal trouble
last evening was that the numer-
GUS encores of the leading numbers
caused the performance to last we'll
on to midnight. Many excellent
musical numbers were on the pro-
gram. Mrs. J. c. Cunningham made
hit in her numbers, "Would You
If You Could?" and "Twinkling
STAR" in which latter she was as
sisted by Prof. Ryan; while Miss
Mcclellan in Take Me Where
There's a Big Brass Band; Miss
Alma Marshall in $50 Long, Mary"
and Mr.. Demert in katy Dear re-
ceived liberal applause and en-
cores. w. w. Tolman in brief
speech prior to the raising Of the
curtain told of the aims of the club
and it's desire to erect a suitable
clubhouse, towards which the pro-
ceeds of 'The Whirl O. tn Town"
will be applied. The performance
will be repeated tonight.


The big musical affair of the
year is promised Friday and Satur-
day nights with Saturday matinee
in Walter Damrosch's New York
Symphony orchestra.
